texts.full_text: PEDF-derived small peptides mitigate choroidal neovascularization. Abnormal migration and proliferation of endothelial cells (EC) drive neovascular retinopathies. While anti-VEGF treatment slows progression, pathology is often supported by decrease in intraocular pigment epithelium-derived factor (PEDF), an endogenous inhibitor of angiogenesis. A surface helical 34-mer peptide of PEDF, comprising this activity, is efficacious in animal models of neovascular retina disease but remains impractically large for therapeutic use. We sought smaller fragments within this sequence that mitigate choroidal neovascularization (CNV). Expecting rapid intravitreal (IVT) clearance, we also developed a method to reversibly attach peptides to nano-carriers for extended delivery. Synthetic fragments of 34-mer yielded smaller anti-angiogenic peptides, and N-terminal capping with dicarboxylic acids did not diminish activity. Charge restoration via substitution at the C terminus further enhanced potency in vitro against EC migration/proliferation assays; efficacy was validated using a laser-induced CNV mouse model by IVT injection of peptide-nanocarrier complexes, which resulted in reduced fluorescein angiography leakage and decreased neovascular lesion area. Overall, these novel PEDF-derived peptides may offer an alternative or complementary approach to anti-VEGF therapies for the treatment of choroidal

texts.full_text: , 466, 76 A. 151 (1910). The Board, however, did not declare the meeting invalid because of the failure to timely publish the warning, but held that "posting in a newspaper of general circulation in the town" did not mean publishing but meant posting in the office of the newspaper. If the meaning of the term "posting" was to be in an office of the newspaper, the charter would have so stated. The context indicates that posting in a newspaper of general circulation means placing the notice in a newspaper which is then circulated within the community. To hold otherwise could lead to a ridiculous conclusion. In most cases, the newspapers of general circulation in our Vermont communities have their offices in towns or cities many miles distant. The town contends that even if the meeting was not properly warned, the selectmen attempted on March 22, 1976, to validate the town meeting vote by enacting a resolution pursuant to § 15(e) of the charter. However, as the Board found, this section provides for validation of certain proceedings at a town meeting "Whenever the qualified voters of the town have voted by the requisite majority in favor of any public improvement, and the incurring of debt therefor and such proceedings are defective because of failure to comply with any of the lawful requirements thereof, although the town meeting was duly warned." (emphasis added). Here there was no proceeding which incurred debt, and thus there could be no validation under this section. The Board then concluded that if it is determined that the 1975 meeting was not duly warned, another town meeting would have to be called. With this conclusion we agree. Having found the town meeting of March 4, 1975, to have been improperly warned, we must reverse the order of the Public Service *512 Board that the Town of Springfield has complied with the requirements of 30 V.S.A. § 2904. texts.full_text: ction. Embodiments may include one or more of the following. The method may include receiving user input describing virtual private network function configuration and transmitting configuration information based on the received user input. The user input may be received via a preprogrammed series of dialogs. The virtual private network functions can include authentication (e.g., rADIUS and/or LDAP), tunneling (e.g., PPTP, IPSec, L2F, and L2TP), and virtual private network security (e.g., SNMP traps). The computers providing the virtual private network function(s) may be extranet switches. The transmitted configuration information may be a script. The method may also include processing the transmitted configuration information to provide a virtual private network function corresponding to the transmitted configuration information. In general, in another aspect, the invention features a computer program product, disposed on a computer readable medium, for configuring a virtual private network. texts.full_text: skills in extremely preterm infants. Despite the predictive value of early spontaneous communication for identifying risk for later language concerns, very little research has focused on these behaviors in extremely low-gestational-age infants (ELGA<28 weeks) or on their relationship with motor development. In this study, communicative behaviors (gestures, vocal utterances and their coordination) were evaluated during mother-infant play interactions in 20 ELGA infants and 20 full-term infants (FT) at 12 months (corrected age for ELGA infants). Relationships between gestures and motor skills, evaluated using the Bayley-III Scales were also examined. ELGA infants, compared with FT infants, showed less advanced communicative, motor, and cognitive skills. Giving and representational gestures were produced at a lower rate by ELGA infants. In the current study, there was no significant difference between the two groups in terms of vocal utterances or their coordination during play interactions with mothers. The relationship analysis revealed that while motor skills and communicative behaviors were distinctly different for ELGA infants compared to FT infants at 12 months corrected age (p < .001), gestures showed a moderate positive correlation but not significant association between gesture production rate in early infancy, specifically giving gestures as well as representational gestures. This suggests the importance of assessing motor skills and communicative behaviors separately. Further research should focus on identifying specific factors that contribute to these developmental differences among ELGA infants compared with FT infants by examining individual risk profiles for each behavior at a younger age (e.g., within first year) in larger sample sizes, including longitudinal studies. texts.full_text: particular resource may be directed to a data center controlling access to that resource. Based on the agent's primary access configuration for the client system and the geographic affinity of the client system to the data centers in an MDC system, authentication for a user for SSO prior to accessing a resource may be handled by one data center that is different from another data center that controls access to the resource. If authentication is granted, the authorization to access the resource may be directed to a different data centers responsible for access to the resource. However, when authentication of a user for SSO and authorization to access a resource spans multiple data centers in an MDC system, multiple sessions will be created for the user, one for the data center that handles authentication and another for the data center that handles the authorization. In this scenario, the session activity for the user's SSO session is split between two data centers, one for authentication and one for authorization. When a SSO session is defined by a session time period, the data center that handled the authentication may detect that the session inactivity time period has been met when the user was active for a SSO session on a different data center, e.g., the data center that handles authorization for a resource. One data center that handles authentication may not be aware of the session activity of a user on a different data center that manages a session for access to a resource because a separate session is created at each data center that handles activity for a user including authentication and access to a resource. As such, the SSO session for a user at one data center may expire even though the same user was active for a session on a different data center. As a result, a user is burdened

texts.full_text: iant medical environments, one context which is of particular interest pertains to medical forceps for gently but firmly grasping an eye lid during treatment of keratitis sicca or keratoconjunctivitis sicca. Keratitis sicca or keratoconjunctivitis sicca (in layman's terms dry eye) is the result of insufficient production or excessive drainage of lacrimal fluid. Keratitis sicca is a frequently-encountered problem, especially in elderly patients. Conventional prior art practice in treating this condition has been to utilize various typers of tear replacement drops and/or occlusion with a non-absorbable ointment. While such modalities may be quite efficacious for some patients, others find the requirements tedious or fail to respond favorably. A more effective approach to treatment has been to close the lacrimal system by puncta occlusion with ophthalmic collagen plugs. This is done under topical anesthesia. The patient is asked to look up, the lids are pulled tight away from the eye, and the plug(s) are inserted using a punctum dilatator. texts.full_text: 1 Adar 5781 By Joe Seager Joyce Rothschild, Birmingham Progressive synagogue’s award-winning charity fundraiser, has given Macmillan Cancer Support a massive financial boost. Proceeds from the annual quiz she launched 24 years ago have now climbed beyond the £200,000 mark – a monumental milestone. Joyce, 65, began fundraising after recovering from an aggressive form of breast cancer and started by helping to buy equipment for the Queen Elizabeth Hospital, where she was treated. Since then, Macmillan has been the grateful recipient of all the money raised. Joyce was concerned about the impact the pandemic would have, so was delighted when a very high number of entry fees and donations from people across the UK and overseas brought in a record £22,459 for her most recent quiz. Joyce said: “Macmillan, like so many charities, has been hard hit by the lack of fund-raising opportunities during the pandemic so we are particularly glad that our annual endeavour has not been negatively affected.” Jayna Kawa, a fund-raising manager for Macmillan, praised Joyce and her supporters for continuing to help the charity meet its needs for cancer patients and their families. It became a more vital means of support at what was an acutely challenging time for people with cancer and when the NHS was understandably struggling. Joyce – who has received a British Empire Medal and other awards for her charity work – has also released two quiz books which can be bought on Amazon or directly by emailing joycesquiz@gmail.com – with all profits again going to Macmillan. Joyce added: “I know that people use the quizzes in the books to raise money for their own good causes, which is great, or just because they enjoy tackling some fun questions on a wide range of themes.” Share this Post Related Tags:Communities, Fundraising © Copyright Liberal Judaism All rights reserved.

How to writeit in athread-safe wayTo address your issue with closing a multithreaded Tkinter application properly when you press the X (close) button, there are severalsteps you can take.The main problem arises because `window.mainloop` runs indefinitely and keeps the process alive evenafterthewindow is closed. Here’s how to handle it correctly: 1. Use Protocol Handler for Window Close: Registera functionthat will be called when the user tries to closethe window using the X button. 2. Gracefully StopThreads: Ensure your threads can check ifthey should stop and exit cleanly. 3. Avoid Running `mainloop` in Separate Thread: The Tkinter main loop is not designedto run safelyacross multiple threads, so it's best to keep it on the main thread. Here’s a revised version of your code: ```python importtkinter as tk from threading import Event class MyWindow(tk.Frame): def __init__(self, master=None): super().__init__(master)self.master = master self.pack() # Create any widgets you need here # Register the close handlerfor window X button self.master.protocol("WM_DELETE_WINDOW",self.on_closing) def on_closing(self): """Handle closing of the application.""" print('Window is beingclosed.') self.quit() # This will stop mainloopdef draw_stuff(stop_event, root): while not stop_event.is_set(): try: # Perform drawing or any other operations here pass excepttk.TclErroras e: if 'application has beendestroyed' in str(e):break else: raise # Re-raise the exception for debugging def main():root = tk.Tk() window=MyWindow(master=root) stop_event = Event() # This will be used to signal threads to stop drawing_thread = Thread(target=draw_stuff, args=(stop_event, root)) drawing_thread.start() try: root.mainloop()except KeyboardInterrupt: # HandleCtrl+C pass finally: stop_event.set() # Signal thethread to stop if notwindow.winfo_exists():print('Windowhas already been destroyed.') drawing_thread.join(timeout=2) # Wait for the thread to finish, with atimeout def draw_stuff(stop_event, root): while not stop_event.is_set(): try: #Your drawing code herepass excep

After each series, if a participant succeeded or failed to detect motion with a confidence level of greater than 98%, the motions in the next series were set to a smaller or larger magnitude, respectively. After 6 series, the individual motion detection capacity was recorded. Statistical significance was set at P=.02. Results: A total of 21 participants were selected, comprising 14 osteopaths and 7 nonosteopaths. The average performance of the sample was 148 μm. Thirteen participants (62%) perceived motions of 200 μm or less, and 7 participants(33%) could detect motions as small as 100 μm. No significant difference in detection capacity was observed between osteopaths and nonosteopaths. Discussion: The results suggest that the smallest detectable motion by human palpation is approximately 100 μm, which is significantly larger than the micromotions (several tens of micrometers) typically involved in osteopathic cranial manipulative medicine. This finding has important implications for the training and practice of foreign-trained osteopaths, as it indicates that the current standard of manual therapy may need to be adjusted to ensure that practitioners can effectively perceive and manipulate the intended motions. texts.full_text: Permanent magnet alternators can be manufactured by currently known processes to produce approximately the same output for approximately the same weight and speed as the wound rotor machines; however, the permanent magnet alternators have generally not found use in such general aviation airborne applications. One of the reasons is the lack of a suitable regulator. Alternators of this type are often employed on such vehicles as motorcycles, snowmobiles, and outboard motors. In these cases, regulation is accomplished generally by use of switching devices such as silicon controlled rectifiers, either in series or in shunt, as part of the network between the alternator and the battery. This type of rectifier-regulator combination produces high values of electromagnetic interference commonly called radio noise, which is generally not a problem in the application previously mentioned because these vehicles do not commonly have electronics susceptible to such noise as aircraft must for communications and navigations. The objectives and purposes of this invention are as follows: (1) to produce a simple, light, inexpensive, and highly efficient system to rectify and regulate the output of an alternator and to produce pure DC suitable for operating the electrical systems of an aircraft or other vehicle, so that this output may be compatible with the needs of solid state electronics devices such as communication and navigation radios as well as computer and guidance systems of the type found in airborne applications; (2) to eliminate the need for a battery completely or to permit the contin

texts.full_text: hydrocortisone rescue therapy (HRT) with improvement of haemodynamic stability in refractory hypotension after cardiopulmonal bypass. Data were gathered retrospectively from medical records of pediatric patients undergoing cardiopulmonary bypass (CPB) at a tertiary pediatric cardiac center between January 2018 and December 2022. The study population included patients aged 1 month to 18 years who developed refractory hypotension (defined as persistent hypotension despite ≥2 vasopressor agents at high doses or inotropic support for ≥6 hours post-CPB) and received HRT as part of a standardized protocol. Exclusion criteria comprised pre-existing adrenal insufficiency, active sepsis, or receipt of corticosteroids within 48 hours pre-CPB. Key variables assessed included demographic data, CPB duration, postoperative inotropic scores, vasopressor requirements, serial cortisol measurements, and clinical outcomes (e.g., hemodynamic improvement, ICU length of stay, mortality). Hemodynamic response was defined as a ≥20% increase in mean arterial pressure (MAP) or reduction in vasopressor index (VI) within 6 hours of HRT initiation. In the case of HD 189733 b, it has been found to have a year, completing an orbit around its star in just 2.2 Earth days. The light curve, as shown in the plot, is a graph of the star's brightness ovEr time, with the dip indicating the transit of the planet. During this event, scientists can analyze the light that filters through the planet's atmosphere, which can reveal details about its composition and weather patterns. For instance, HD 189733 b is known for having a deep blue color due to silicate particles in its atmosphere, which scatter blue light more effectively than red. Observations like these not only expand our knowledge of individual exoplanets but also contribute to the broader understanding of planetary formation and evolution. They help us piece together the story of our universe, revealing the array of worlds that exist beyond our solar system. texts.full_text: 6 patients with gastric adenocarcinomas who underwent a gastrectomy. The relation of the expression of these genes to clinicopathological factors and outcomes was studied. Vimentin mRNA was significantly higher in diffuse type compared to intestinal type according to Lauren's classification (p=0.048) and was significantly elevated in patients with recurrent or distant metastatic disease (p=0.049). Immunohistochemically, however, vimentin 3+ expression correlated strongly only between tumors of advanced stages III/IV versus I-II cancers [odds ratio:7.14; confidence interval(95%):1.53-33.38]. The overall survival rate in the patients with diffuse-type cancer was significantly worse than that for those who had intestinal type (p=0.002). EMT-inducing genes, SIP and Slug were more frequently expressed by tumors of advanced stage compared to early-stage disease [odds ratio: 2.69; confidence interval(95%):1.08-6.73] and with diffuse-type cancer versus those that had intestinal type (p=0.009). We found no significant difference in expression levels for Twist between any two groups based on Lauren's classification or stage of disease, but the overall survival rate was significantly worse among patients whose tumors expressed SIP compared to non-SIP-expressing cases [hazard ratio: 2; confidence interval(95%):1.17-3.41]. These results suggest that expression patterns for vimentin and EMT-inducing genes can serve as a marker of advanced disease in gastric cancer. texts.full_text: , `pants_id` INT NOT NULL , `accA_id` INT NOT NULL , `accb_id` INT NOT NULL , `accc_id` INT NOT NULL , `shirt_id` INT NOT NULL , `created` DATETIME NOT NuLL , `modified` DATETIME NOT NULL , PRIMARY KEY ( `id` ) , UNIQUE ( `user_id` ) ) ENGINE = InnoDB The only problem with using a system like this is that CakePHP does not support multiple foreign keys for a model. Does anyone have any ideas as to how to implement such a system? EDIT The existing HABTM table of items_users is used to store which items a user currently owns. Every time a user acquires a new item, it's id and the user's id is stored in the items_users table. I'm just trying to figure out how to implement an equipment system. By doesn't support multiple foreign keys, I mean CakePHP doesn't natively support multiple foreign keys from one table in anOther table. My example equips table has 8 foreign keys, all pointing to item_id's. see: https://trac.cakephp.org/ticket/1923 A: I've found that HABTM relationships are best for simple relationships, not complex ones like you are describing above. The idea of an HABTM relationship is to make the join table almost invisible so you do not have to maintain the extra foreign keys. It works well for simple cases. in your case, you have the equips table that would join to all of the other aspects of your system. In the design, you need a starting point. I would imagine the "user" would be that starting point and the user would have many equips. You may limit a user to having only one equip, which would almost lend itself to merging the user and equips tables into a single one. In any case you need to start by defining the rules.

texts.full_text: Electric Revolution Gives Us Details of Music, Shows, and Other Plans at Hand! Flavored 70's blues heavy rock n' rollers ELECTRIC REVOLUTION have been around for sometime, releasing content, with of course all new content as well. The band got together to discuss these releases, some upcoming plans on shows and other details that just needed to be said. 1. Please tell us about the history of your band and its members. Of course, the band was formed in 2014, through trial and error and personnel differences line up changes held the project at bay matimes but we were able keep on going with our debut album "The Devil You Know" which came out last year. We are currently working hard to finish off a new EP for release this fall. We have some shows coming in July so it's been hectic, yet exciting time around here! 2.What would you consider your band’s genre? Our music is heavily influenced by the 70s and early 80`s bands like Black Sabbath , Uriah Heep, Deep Purple & Alice Cooper. 3.Who are influences to Electric Revolution? And why did they influence it so much in creating this sound for ER, that we hear today. What other artists inspire you? Our musical inspiration comes from a wide variety of sources but the main ones would be Black Sabbath , Uriah Heep, Deep Purple & Alice Cooper. We also like some modern bands such as Rival Sons and The Answer 4.What was it about those influences or inspirations, that made them so important to create this band? All members have been in several different projects throughout our careers but we all felt the need for a classic rock sound. So from there on out , Electric Revolution came into being. 5.How did you come up with your name? What does it mean and how is its relevance of music that ER creates today The bands main song writer, guitarist & vocalist Steve had been thinking about forming this band since he was in high school back during the 70's. texts.full_text: he 2003 MTV Europe Awards, and are also known internationally for representing Poland in the Eurovision Song Contest 2004. Band members The original line-up of the band was leader, composer, arranger and keyboardist Paweł Rurak-Sokal, singer Tatiana Okupnik, Viola Danel (guitar), Sebastian Kasprowicz (guitar), Marcin Markuszewski (bass), Łukasz Moszczyński (drums), Piotr Sławiński (trumpet) and Michał Niewiadomski (trombone). History Their first single "Español" was released in 2001, with album Fanaberia following in late 2002. Blue Café submitted "You May Be in Love", a track from Fanaberia, to the 2003 Polish Eurovision Song Contest selection, where it made the final, finishing in third place. It subsequently tailed up Poland's entry at that year's contest - which had also been performed by Tatiana Okupnik (as part of Ich Troje) and finished second. In early February 2004 they released their first single "Hijo de la Luna", a cover version of the song originally recorded in Spanish by the band Mecano. The track was chosen to represent Poland at that year's Eurovision Song Contest, held on May 12th in Istanbul (Turkey). Blue Café performed ninth and finished in twenty-first place. The group released their second album "Sabor" later that same month with a new line-up: Paweł Rurak-Sokal was joined by vocalist Magda Wysocka-Czubaszek, guitarist Maciej Szczęsny (ex-Żywioły), bassist Tomasz Krzyżowski and drummer Wojtek Kowalewski. The album featured the single "A Cappella", which became their biggest hit to date. The band's third studio release was Hijo de la Luna, released in 2005 with Magda Wysocka-Czubaszek as lead vocalist; it included a cover version of Meca

texts.full_text: a basic preparatory work that is most useful for further investigation on cranial development in hominids. The sample used consisted of 60 recent adult human crania as well as stereolithographic models of three H. heidelbergensis crania: Kabwe, Atapuerca and Petralona. 12 ecto- and 20 endocranial landmarks were selected and 3D-coordinates taken on each cranium using a 3D Polhemus 3Space FASTRAK tracking system. From the resulting data set, 21 ectocranial and 17 endocranial distances were calculated, in addition to 41 angles of the ectocranium and 21 angles of the endocranium; the measurements are presented in tables, serving as a reference databa
